claim: A discovery-memory claim should only advance when evidence, variables, privacy boundary, falsification route, and next action are all explicit.
falsification_route: If promoted packets repeatedly lack reproducible tests or cannot be converted into executable fixtures, the matrix is too permissive.
next_executable_action: Run the matrix after evidence review and before prototype planning.

claim: A packet containing raw private observation text should be stored in public discovery memory.
falsification_route: Any private-risk packet must fail promotion regardless of other fields.
next_executable_action: Redact or transform into a deidentified packet before review.

claim: Symbolic observations can be translated into operational research variables.
falsification_route: If independent reviewers cannot map the same symbolic packet to compatible operational variables, the translation rule fails.

claim: A hypothesis gate can promote claims without checking failed-result ledgers.
falsification_route: If contradiction-bearing packets are promoted, the promotion matrix is unsafe.
contradictions: Existing contradiction ledger requires triage before promotion.
next_executable_action: Route to contradiction review before any promotion.

claim: The system understands all future discovery contexts.
